patch.value: Canonicalize dual beads/todos package implementations and define external-package dependency-range policy

Canonical home decided per package (extend an existing implementation, never a third); non-canonical repo README points to the canonical package and repo is archived or repurposed; capability divergence documented (external pm-beads import+export+validate+diff+bead_id schema vs in-repo import-only) with migration note; ecosystem dependency-range convention documented (caret year-range with verified floor; no exact pins, no unbounded >=) and pm-ops exact-pin 2026.7.13 plus pm-beads >=2026.7.10 ranges fixed; floor-verification folded into the reusable ecosystem smoke harness or the pm-o8um Verified-tier checklist

## Evidence (review pass #90, 2026-07-15)

Two pm extension packages exist as TWO independent live implementations each, with no canonical pointer:

- **beads**: external npm `pm-beads@2026.7.13` (unscoped, repo unbraind/pm-beads, installs via `pm install github.com/unbraind/pm-beads --global`; capabilities: import + export + `beads-validate`/`beads-diff` commands + `bead_id` schema field) vs in-repo `packages/pm-beads` (`@unbrained/pm-beads@2026.7.15`, private:true, first-party catalog, import-only per README). Divergent feature sets, divergent versions, different package identities.
- **todos**: external npm `pm-todos@2026.7.11` (unscoped, repo unbraind/pm-todos) vs in-repo `packages/pm-todos` (`@unbrained/pm-todos@2026.7.15`, private:true). Closed items pm-13bn (registerImporter/Exporter migration) and pm-ybfj (beads/todos runtime dedupe) only ever touched the in-repo pair.

Agents/users can install either implementation and get different behavior; fixes land in one and not the other.

## Dependency-range spread across the 18 external pm-* repos (verified 2026-07-15)

- Floors span `^2026.7.5` → `^2026.7.13`; floors are not verified against the API surface actually used (caret on date-based versions floats within the year, so most resolve to latest — the floor is documentation, currently unaudited).
- `pm-ops` pins exact `2026.7.13` (never floats → guaranteed staleness).
- external `pm-beads` uses unbounded `>=2026.7.10` (silently crosses the 2027 year-major boundary).

## Fix direction

Pick the canonical home per package (extend, never a third implementation), archive/redirect the other, document capability parity + migration. Define one ecosystem range convention (caret year-range with a floor verified by the pm-vnjh reusable smoke harness or the pm-o8um Verified-tier checks) and fix the two outliers.

## Cleared this pass (do not re-investigate)

External consumers (pm-graph/pm-web/pm-github/pm-changelog + companion examples) import ONLY official subpaths (`./sdk`, `./sdk/runtime`, `./sdk/testing`) — no deep `dist/` imports anywhere in the org; pm-graph's SDK imports are type-only.

author: codex-audit-20260715

patch.value: Choose one canonical implementation each for Beads and Todos integrations, redirect or retire duplicates, and enforce one verified pm-cli dependency-range policy across all external ecosystem packages.

The current external dependency-range split and the duplicated in-repo versus external beads/todos capability families remain the focused policy concerns already owned here; no duplicate ecosystem item created.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Range-policy evidence refresh (2026-07-17 ecosystem check of externals updated since the 07-15 sweep): pm-github 2026.7.17 peer >=2026.7.14; pm-presets 2026.7.17 peer >=2026.7.10; pm-context peer >=2026.7.10; pm-web 2026.7.17-1 DIRECT dep ^2026.7.15; pm-brief direct ^2026.7.11; pm-slack-standup direct ^2026.7.5. The peer-vs-direct-dependency split and unbounded >= floors persist in the newest packages — the policy this item owns should also prescribe WHERE @unbrained/pm-cli belongs (peerDependencies for extensions loaded in-process; direct dependency only for standalone hosts like pm-web). No collision found between external pm-graph (namespaced `pm-graph *` commands, Neo4j sync) and the new native `pm graph` command.
